name: Asid ibn Malik
title: Trampler of the Body
shortDescription: One of the ten horsemen who cruelly trampled their horses over Imam Hussain's (AS) headless body after his martyrdom.
fullStory:
Usayd ibn Malik was one of the symbols of cruelty and hard-heartedness in the Kufan army. Willing to do anything to please Ibn Ziyad and get a meager worldly reward, he participated in Ashura's most heinous crime.

When Umar Sa'd asked for volunteers to trample Imam Hussain's (AS) body with horses, Asid ibn Malik volunteered along with 9 others (like Akhnas ibn Marthad). They put fresh horseshoes on their horses to inflict harder blows.

These ten shamelessly trampled their horses over the holy body of the Master of Martyrs (AS), crushing the bones of his chest and back. Asid ibn Malik then went to Ibn Ziyad and, by reciting poetry, proudly expressed his crime, but was met with a cold reaction and a meager reward.

During Mukhtar al-Thaqafi's uprising, Asid ibn Malik was captured. In retaliation for his crime, Mukhtar ordered his hands and feet to be tied, and had horses trample his body until he died.
quoteArabic: أَمَرَ المُخْتارُ بِهِمْ فَشُدُّوا فِي الحَدِيدِ وَأُوطِئُوا الخَيْلَ حَتّى هَلَكُوا...
quote: (His fate in history:) Mukhtar ordered them to be bound in iron and horses trampled them until they perished.
